author: Augustine of Hippo
book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.

classification.cautionReason: No major mainstream heresy warning is attached by the preview classifier.

lineage: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.

ccel.title: NPNF1-08. St. Augustine: Exposition on the Book of Psalms

source.title: NPNF1-08. St. Augustine: Exposition on the Book of Psalms

title: Psalm XCVII

source.title: NPNF1-08. St. Augustine: Exposition on the Book of Psalms

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: 1. …This Psalm is entitled, “A Psalm of David’s, when his land was restored.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Let us refer the whole to Christ, if we wish to keep the road of a right understanding: let us not depart from the corner stone, lest our understanding suffer a fall: in Him let that become fixed, which wavered with unstable motion; let that rest upon Him, which before was waving to and fro in uncertainty.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Whatever doubt a man hath in his mind when he heareth the Scriptures of God, let him not depart from Christ; when Christ hath been revealed to him in the words, let him then be assured that he hath understood; but before he arriveth at the understanding of Christ, let him not presume that he hath understood.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “For Christ is the end of the law for righteousness to every one that believeth.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What doth this mean, and how are these words understood in Christ, “When his land was restored”?…

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The earth restored is the resurrection of the flesh; for after His resurrection, all those things which are sung of in the Psalm were done.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Let us then hear a Psalm full of joy on the restoration of the Earth.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Let the Lord our God excite in us a hope and a pleasure worthy of so great a thing; may He rule our discourse, that it be fit for your hearts, that whatever joy our heart doth feel in such sights, He may bring on to our tongue, and thence conduct it into your ears, then to your heart, thence to your actions.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: 3. …“The Lord is King, let the earth be glad: yea, let the multitude of the isles be joyous” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: It is so indeed, because the word of God hath been preached not in the continent alone, but also in those isles which lie in mid sea: even these are full of Christians, full of the servants of God.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For the sea doth not retard Him who made it.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Where ships can approach, cannot the words of God?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The isles are filled.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But figuratively the isles may be taken for all the Churches.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Because the waves of all temptations roar around them.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But as an isle may be beaten by the waves which on every side dash around it, yet cannot be broken, and rather itself doth break the advancing waves, than by them is broken: so also the Churches of God, springing up throughout the world, have suffered the persecutions of the ungodly, who roar around them on every side; and behold the isles stand fixed, and at last the sea is calmed.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Clouds and darkness are round about Him: righteousness and judgment are the direction of His seat” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: 2).…The Lord Himself saith: “For judgment I am come into this world; that they which see not might see, and that they which see might be made blind.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: They who seem unto themselves to see, who think themselves wise, who think healing not needful for them, that they may be made blind, may not understand.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And that “they which see not may see;” that they who confess their blindness may obtain to be enlightened.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Let there be therefore “clouds and darkness round about Him,” for those who have not understood Him: for those who confess and humble themselves, “righteousness and judgment are the direction of His seat.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He called those who believe in Him His seat: for from them hath He made Himself a seat, since in them Wisdom sitteth; for the Son of God is the Wisdom of God.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But we have heard from another passage of Scripture a strong confirmation of this interpretation.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “The soul of the righteous is the seat of Wisdom.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Because then they who have believed in Him have been made righteous: justified by faith, they have become His own seat: He sitteth in them, judging from them, and guiding them.…

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “There shall go a fire before Him, and burn up His enemies on every side” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: We remember having read in the Gospel, He shall say, “Depart into everlasting fire, prepared for the devil and his angels.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: I do not think it is said of that fire.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Why do I not?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Because he speaketh of some fire, which shall go before Him, before He cometh to judgment.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For it is said, that the fire goeth before Him, and burneth up His enemies on every side, that is, throughout the whole world.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: That fire will burn after His advent: this, on the contrary, will go before Him.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What fire then is this?…Behold, we have understood the fire that goeth before Him, that is to be understood of a kind of temporal punishment of the unbelieving and ungodly: let us understand the fire, if possible, of the salvation of the redeemed also; for thus we had proposed.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The Lord Himself saith: “I am come to send fire on the earth:” “fire” in the same way as a “sword;” as in another passage He saith, that He was not come to send peace, but a sword, upon earth.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The sword to divide, the fire to burn: but each salutary: for the sword of His own word hath in salutary wise separated us from evil habits.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For He brought a sword, and separated every believer either from his father who believed not in Christ, or from his mother in like manner unbelieving: or at least, if we were born of Christian parents, from his ancestors.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For no man among us had not either a grandsire, or great grandsire, or some ancestry among the heathen, and in that unbelief which is accursed before God.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: We are separated from that which we were before; but the sword which separateth, but slayeth not, hath cut between us.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: In the same way the fire also: “I am come to send fire upon the earth.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Believers in Him were set on fire, they received the flame of love: and for this reason when the Holy Spirit itself had been sent to the Apostles, It thus appeared: “cloven tongues, like as of fire.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Burning with this fire they set out on their march through the world, to burn and set on fire His enemies on every side.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What enemies of His?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: They who forsaking the God who made them, adored the idols they had made.…

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “His lightnings gave shine unto the world” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: This is great joy.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Do we not see? is it not clear?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: His lightnings have shined unto the whole world: His enemies have been set on fire, and burnt.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: All that gainsaid hath been burnt, and “His lightnings have given shine unto the world.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: How have they shone?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: That the world might at length believe.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Whence were the lightnings?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: From the clouds.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What are the clouds of God?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The preachers of the truth.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But thou seest a cloud, misty and dark in the sky, and it hath I know not what hidden within it.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: If there be lightning from the cloud, a brightness shineth forth: from that which thou didst despise, hath burst forth that which thou mayest dread.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Our Lord Jesus Christ therefore sent His Apostles, as His preachers, like clouds: they were seen as men, and were despised; as clouds appear, and are despised, until what thou wonderest at gleameth from them.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For they were in the first place men encumbered with flesh, weak; then, men of low station, unlearned, ignoble: but there was within what could lighten forth; there was in them what could flash abroad.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Peter a fisherman approached, prayed, and the dead arose.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: His human form was a cloud, the splendour of the miracle was the lightning.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: So in their words, so in their deeds, when they do things to be wondered at, and utter words to be wondered at, “His lightnings gave shine unto the world; the earth saw it, and was afraid.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Is it not true?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Doth not the whole Christian world at length exclaim, Amen, afraid at the lightnings which burst forth from those clouds?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “The hills melted like wax at the presence of the Lord” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Who are the hills?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Every high thing raising itself against God, at the deeds of Christ and of the Christians, trembled, yielded, and when I say, what hath been already said, “melted,” a better word cannot be found.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “The hills melted like wax at the presence of the Lord.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Where is the elevation of powers? where the hardness of the unbelieving?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The Lord was a fire unto them, they melted at His presence like wax; so long hard, until that fire was applied.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Every height hath been levelled; it dareth not now blaspheme Christ: and though the Pagan believeth not in Him, he blasphemeth Him not; though not as yet become a living stone, yet the hard hill hath been subdued.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “At the presence of the Lord of the whole earth:” not of the Jews only, but of the Gentiles also, as the Apostle saith; for He is not the God of the Jews alone, but of the Gentiles also.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He is therefore the Lord of the whole earth, the Lord Jesus Christ born in Judæa, but not born for Judæa alone, because before He was born He created all men; and He who created, also new created, all men.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “The heavens have declared His righteousness: and all the people have seen His glory” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What heavens have declared?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “The heavens declare the glory of God.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Who are the heavens?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Those who have become His seat; for as God sitteth in the heavens, so doth He sit in the Apostles, so doth He sit in the preachers of the Gospel.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Even thou, if thou wilt, shalt be a heaven.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Dost thou wish to be so?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Purge from thy heart the earth.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: If thou hast not earthly lusts, and hast not in vain uttered the response, that thou hast “lifted up thy heart,” thou shalt be a heaven.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “If ye be risen with Christ,” saith the Apostle to believers, “set your affection on things above, not on things of the earth.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Thou hast begun to set thine affection upon things above, not on things upon earth; hast thou not become a heaven?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Thou carriest flesh, and in thy heart thou art already a heaven; for thy conversation will be in heaven.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Being such, thou also declarest Christ; for who of the faithful declareth not Christ?…Therefore the whole Church preacheth Christ, and the heavens declare His righteousness; for all the faithful, whose care it is to gain unto God those who have not yet believed, and who do this from love, are heavens.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: From them God thundereth forth the terror of His judgment; and he who was unbelieving trembleth, and is alarmed, and believeth.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He shows unto men what power Christ had throughout the world, by pleading with them, and leading them to love Christ.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For how many this day have led their friends either to some pantomimist, or flute-player?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Why, except from their liking him?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And do ye love Christ.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For He who conquered the world hath exhibited such spectacles, as that no man can say that he findeth in them cause for blame.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For each person’s favourite in the theatre is often vanquished there.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But no man is vanquished in Christ: there is no reason for shame.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Seize, lead, draw, whom ye may: be without fear, ye are leading unto Him, who displeaseth not those who see Him; and ask ye Him to enlighten them, that they may behold to good account.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Confounded be all they that worship carved images” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Hath not this come to pass?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Have they not been confounded?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Are they not daily confounded?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For carved images are images wrought by the hand.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Why are all who worship carved images confounded?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Because all people have seen His glory.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: All nations now confess the glory of Christ: let those who worship stones be ashamed.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Because those stones were dead, we have found a living Stone; indeed those stones never lived, so that they cannot be called even dead; but our Stone is living, and hath ever lived with the Father, and though He died for us, He revived, and liveth now, and death shall no more have dominion over Him.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: This glory of His the nations have acknowledged; they leave the temples, they run to the Churches.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Do they still seek to worship carved images?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Have they not chosen to forsake their idols?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: They have been forsaken by their idols.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Who glory in their idols.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But there is a certain disputer who seemeth unto himself learned, and saith, I do not worship that stone, nor that image which is without sense;…I worship not this image but I adore what I see, and serve him whom I see not.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Who is that?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Some invisible deity, he replieth, who presideth over that image.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: By giving this account of their images, they seem to themselves able disputants, because they do not worship idols, and yet do worship devils.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “The things,” brethren, saith the Apostle, “which the Gentiles sacrifice, they sacrifice unto devils, and not to God; we know that an idol is nothing: and that what the Gentiles sacrifice, they sacrifice to devils, and not to God; and I would not that ye should have fellowship with devils.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Let them not therefore excuse themselves on this ground, that they are not devoted to insensate idols; they are rather devoted to devils, which is more dangerous.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For if they were only worshipping idols, as they would not help them, so they would not hurt them; but if thou worship and serve devils, they themselves will be thy masters.…

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But observe holy men, who are like the Angels.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: When thou hast found some holy man who serveth God, if thou wish to worship him instead of God, he forbiddeth thee: he will not arrogate to himself the honour due to God, he will not be unto thee as God, but be with thee under God.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Thus did the holy Apostles Paul and Barnabas.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: They preached the word of God in Lycaonia.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: When they had performed wonderful works in Lycaonia, the people of that country brought victims, and wished to sacrifice to them, calling Barnabas Jupiter, and Paul Mercury: they were not pleased.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Did they perchance refuse to be sacrificed to, because they abhorred to be compared to devils?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: No, but because they shuddered at divine honour being paid to men.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Their own words show this: it is no guess of ours; for the text of the book goeth on to say how they were moved.…Just then, as good men forbade those who had wished to worship them as gods, and wish rather that God alone be worshipped, God alone be adored, to God alone sacrifice be offered, not to themselves; so also all the holy Angels seek His glory whom they love; endeavour to impel and to excite to the contemplation of Him all whom they love: Him they declare to them, not themselves, since they are angels; and because they are soldiers, they study only how to seek the glory of their Captain; but if they have sought their own glory, they are condemned as usurpers.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Such were the devil and his angels; he claimed for himself divine honour, and for all his demons; he filled the Pagan temples, and persuaded them to offer images and sacrifices to himself.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Was it not better to worship holy Angels than devils?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: They answer: we do not worship devils; we worship angels, as ye call them, the powers and the ministers of the great God.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: I wish ye would worship them: ye would easily learn from themselves not to worship them.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Hear an Angel teaching.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He was teaching a disciple of Christ, and showing him many wonders in the Revelation of John: and when some wonderful vision had been shown him, he trembled, and fell down at the Angel’s feet; but that Angel, who sought not but the glory of God, said, “See thou do it not; for I am a fellow-servant of thee, and of thy brethren the prophets.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What then, my brethren?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Let no man say, I fear lest the Angel may be angry with me, if I worship him not as my God.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He is then angry with thee, when thou hast chosen to worship him: for he is righteous, and loveth God.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: As devils are angry if they are not worshipped, so are Angels angry if they are worshipped instead of God.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But lest the weak and trembling heart perchance say unto itself: If then the demons are incensed because they are not worshipped, I fear to offend them; what can even their chief the devil do unto thee?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: If he had any power over us no one of us would remain.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Are not daily so many things said against him by the mouth of Christians, and yet the harvest of Christians increaseth.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: When thou art angry with the most depraved of thy slaves, thou givest him the name, “Satan,” Devil.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Perhaps in this thou dost err, since thou sayest it to a man, and thy immoderate anger hurrieth thee to revile the image of God: and yet thou choosest a term thou deeply hatest, to apply to him.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: If he could, would he not revenge himself?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But it is not allowed: and he doth so much only as is allowed him.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For when he wished to tempt Job, he had to ask power to do so: and he could do nothing had he not received power.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Why then dost thou not fearlessly worship God, without whose will no one hurteth thee, and by whose permission thou art chastened, not overcome?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For if it shall have pleased the Lord thy God to permit some man to hurt thee, or some spirit: He will chasten thee, that thou mayest cry unto Him: “Confounded,” therefore, “be all they that delight in vain gods: worship Him, all ye His angels.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Let Pagans learn to worship God: they wish to worship Angels: let them imitate Angels, and worship Him who is worshipped by Angels.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Worship Him, all ye His angels.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Let that Angel worship who was sent to Cornelius (for worshipping Him he sent Cornelius to Peter), himself Peter’s fellow-servant; let him worship Christ, Peter’s Lord.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Worship Him, all ye gods!”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Sion heard of it, and rejoiced” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What did Sion hear?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: That all His Angels worship Him.…For the Church was not as yet among the Gentiles; in Judæa the Jews had some of them believed, and the very Jews who believed thought that they only belonged to Christ: the Apostles were sent to the Gentiles, Cornelius was preached to; Cornelius believed, was baptized, and they who were with Cornelius were also baptized.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But ye know what happened, that they might be baptized: the reader indeed hath not reached this point, but, nevertheless, some recollect; and let those who do not recollect, hear briefly from me.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The Angel was sent to Cornelius: the Angel sent Cornelius to Peter; Peter came to Cornelius.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And because Cornelius and his household were Gentiles, and uncircumcised: lest they might hesitate to give the Gospel to the uncircumcised: before Cornelius and his household were baptized, the Holy Spirit came, and filled them, and they began to speak with tongues.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Now the Holy Spirit had not fallen upon any one who had not been baptized: but upon these It fell before baptism.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For Peter might hesitate whether he might baptize the uncircumcised: the Holy Spirit came, they began to speak with tongues; the invisible gift was given, and took away all doubt about the visible Sacrament; they were all baptized.…What did Sion hear, and rejoice at?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: That the Gentiles also had received the word of God.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: One wall had come, but the corner existed not as yet.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The name Sion is here peculiarly given to the Church which was in Judæa.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Sion heard of it, and rejoiced: and the daughters of Judah were glad.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Thus it is written, “The apostles and brethren that were in Judæa heard.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: See if the daughters of Judæa rejoiced not.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What did they hear?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “That the Gentiles had also received the word of God.”…Therefore, “The daughters of Judah rejoiced because of Thy judgments, O Lord.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What is, because of Thy judgments?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Because in any nation, and in any people, he that serveth Him is accepted of Him: for He is not the God of the Jews only, but also of the Gentiles.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: See if this be not the reason for the joy of the daughters of Judah.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “For Thou, Lord, art most high over all the earth” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Not in Judæa alone, but over Jerusalem; not over Sion only, but over all the earth.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: To this whole earth the judgments of God prevailed, so that it assembled its nations from every quarter: judgments with which they who have cut themselves off have no communion: they neither hear the prophecy, nor see its completion; “For Thou, Lord, art most high over all the earth: Thou art exalted far above all gods.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What is “far”?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For it is said of Christ.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What then meaneth “far,” except that Thou mayest be acknowledged coequal with the Father?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What meaneth, “above all gods”?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Who are they?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Idols have not life, have not sense: devils have life and sense; but they are evil.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What great thing is it that Christ is exalted above devils?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He is exalted above devils: but neither is this very great; the heathen gods indeed are devils, but “He is far above all gods.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Even men are styled gods: “I have said, Ye are gods: and ye are all the children of the Most Highest:” again it is written, “God standeth in the congregation of princes: He is a Judge among gods.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Jesus Christ our Lord is exalted above all: not only above idols, not only above devils; but above all righteous men.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Even this is not enough; above all Angels also: for whence otherwise is this, “Worship Him, all ye gods”?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Thou art far exalted above all gods.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What then do we all, who have assembled before Him, before Him who is exalted far above all gods?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He hath given us a brief commandment, “O ye that love the Lord, see that ye hate the thing which is evil!” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Christ doth not deserve that with Him thou shouldest love avarice.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Thou lovest Him: thou shouldest hate what He hateth.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: There is a man who is thine enemy, he is what thou art; ye are the work of one Creator, with the same nature: and yet if thy son were to speak unto thine enemy, and come to his house, and constantly converse with him, thou wouldest be inclined to disinherit him; because he speaketh with thine enemy.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Because thou seemest to say justly, Thou art my enemy’s friend, and seekest thou aught of my property?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Attend then.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Thou lovest Christ: avarice is Christ’s foe; why speak with her?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: I say not, speak with her; why dost thou serve her?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For Christ commandeth thee to do many things, and thou dost them not; she commandeth thee, and thou dost them.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Christ commandeth thee to clothe the poor man: and thou dost it not; avarice biddeth thee defraud, and this thou dost in preference.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: If such be the case, if such thou art, do not very confidently promise thyself Christ’s heritage.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But thou sayest, I love Christ.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Hence it appeareth that thou lovest what is good, if thou shalt be found to hate what is evil.…

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Because then he had said above, “see that ye hate the thing which is evil,” lest ye should fear to hate evil, lest he should kill thee, he addeth instantly, “The Lord preserveth the souls of His servants.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Hear Him preserving the souls of His servants, and saying, “Fear not them which kill the body, but are not able to kill the soul.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He who hath most power against thee, slayeth the body.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What hath he done unto thee?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What he also did to the Lord thy God.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Why lovest thou to have what Christ hath, if thou fearest to suffer what Christ did?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He came to bear thy life, temporal, weak, subject unto death.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Surely fear to die, if thou canst avoid dying.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What thou canst not avoid through thy nature, why dost thou not undergo by faith?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Let the adversary who threateneth take away from thee that life, God giveth thee another life: for He gave thee this life also, and without His will even this shall not be taken from thee; but if it be His will that it be taken from thee, He hath a life to give thee in exchange; fear not to be robbed for His sake.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Art thou unwilling to put off a patched garment?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He will give thee a robe of glory.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What robe dost thou tell me of?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “This corruptible must put on incorruption, and this mortal must put on immortality.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: This very flesh of thine shall not perish.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Thine enemy can rage as far as to thy death: he hath not power beyond, either over thy soul, or even over thy flesh; for although he scatter thy flesh about, he hindereth not the resurrection.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Men were fearful for their life: and what said the Lord unto them?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “The very hairs of your head are all numbered.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Dost thou, who losest not a single hair, fear the loss of thy life?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: All things are numbered with God.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He who created all things, will restore all things.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: They were not, and they were created: they were, and shall they not be restored?…“He shall deliver them from the hand of the ungodly.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But perhaps thou wilt say, I lose this light.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “There is sprung up a light for the righteous” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What light fearest thou thou mayest lose? fearest thou thou mayest be in darkness?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Fear not thou mayest lose light; nay, fear lest while thou art guarding against the loss of this light, thou mayest lose that true light.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For we see to whom that light is given which thou fearest losing, and with whom it is shared.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Do the righteous only see this sun, when He maketh it rise over the just and unjust, and raineth upon the just and unjust?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Wicked men, robbers, the unchaste, beasts, flies, worms, see that light together with thee.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What sort of light doth He keep for the righteous, who giveth this even to such as these?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Deservedly the Martyrs beheld this light in faith; for they who despised this light of the sun, had some light in their eyes, which they longed for, who rejected this.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Do you imagine that they were really in misery, when they walked in chains?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Spacious was the prison to the faithful, light were the chains to the confessors.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: They who preached Christ amid their torments, had joy in the iron-chair.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What light hath sprung up for the righteous?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Not that which springeth up for the unrighteous; not that which He causeth to rise over the good and bad.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: There is a different light which springeth up to the righteous; of which light, that never rose upon themselves, the unrighteous shall in the end say, “Therefore have we erred from the way of truth, and the light of righteousness hath not shined upon us, and the sun of righteousness rose not upon us.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Behold, by loving this sun they have lain in the darkness of the heart.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What did it profit them to have seen with their eyes this sun, and not in mind to have seen that light?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Tobit was blind, but he used to teach his son the way of God.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Ye know this, that Tobit warned his son, and said to him, “Son, give alms of thy substance; because that alms suffer not to come into darkness.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Even he who was in darkness spoke thus.…Dost thou wish to know that light?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Be true-hearted.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What is, be true-hearted?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Be not of a crooked heart before God, withstanding His will, and wishing to bend Him unto thee, and not to rule thyself to please Him; and thou wilt feel the joyful gladness which all the true-hearted know.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Be glad, ye righteous” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Perhaps already the faithful hearing the word, “Be glad,” are thinking of banquets, preparing cups, waiting for the season of roses; because it is said, “Be glad, ye righteous!”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: See what followeth, “Be glad in the Lord.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Thou art waiting for the season of spring, that thou mayest be glad: thou hast the Lord for joyful gladness, the Lord is always with thee, He hath no special season; thou hast Him by night, thou hast Him by day.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Be true-hearted; and thou hast ever joy from Him.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For that joy which is after the fashion of the world, is not true joy.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Hear the prophet Isaiah: “There is no joy, saith my God, to the wicked.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What the wicked call joy is not joy, such as he knew who made no account of their joy: let us believe him, brethren.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He was a man, but he knew both kinds of joy.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He certainly knew the joys of the cup, for he was a man, he knew the joy of the table, he knew the joys of marriage, he knew those joys worldly and luxurious.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He who knew them saith with confidence, “There is no joy to the wicked, saith the Lord.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But it is not man who speaks, it is the Lord.…But thou sayest, I see not that light which Isaiah saw.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Believe, and thou shalt see it.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For perhaps thou hast not the eye to see it; for it is an eye by which that beauty is discerned.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For as there is an eye of the flesh, by means of which this light is seen: so there is an eye of the heart, by which that joy is perceived: perhaps that eye is wounded, dimmed, disturbed by passion, by avarice, by indulgence, by senseless lust; thine eye is disturbed: thou canst not see that light.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Believe, before thou seest: thou shalt be healed, and shalt see.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “And confess to the remembrance of His holiness.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Now made glad, now rejoicing in the Lord, confess unto Him; for unless it were His will, ye would not rejoice in Him.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For the Lord Himself saith: “These things I have spoken to you: that in Me ye might have peace.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But in the world ye shall have tribulation.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: If ye are Christians, look for tribulations in this world; look not for more peaceful and better times.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Brethren, ye deceive yourselves; what the Gospel doth not promise you, promise not to yourselves.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Ye know what the Gospel saith; we are speaking to Christians; we ought not to disobey the faith.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The Gospel saith this, that in the last times many evils, many stumbling-blocks, many tribulations, much iniquity, shall abound; but he that shall endure unto the end, the same shall be saved.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “The love,” it saith, “of many shall wax cold.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Whosoever then hath been stedfastly fervent in spirit, as the Apostle saith, “fervent in spirit,” his love shall not wax cold: because “the love of God is shed abroad in our hearts by the Holy Ghost, which is given unto us.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Let no man therefore promise himself what the Gospel doth not promise.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Behold, happier times will come, and I am doing this, and purchasing this.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: It is good for thee to listen to Him who is not deceived, nor hath deceived any man, who promised thee joy not here, but in Himself; and when all here hath passed away, to hope that with Him thou wilt for ever reign; lest when thou dost wish to reign here, thou mayest neither enjoy gladness here, nor find it there.
